output_text: A 7-year-old British schoolgirl, India Mayhew, has died after being thrown from a horse while on holiday in India with her family. The incident occurred at a riding facility in Matheran, 20 miles east of Mumbai, when the horse suddenly bolted and dragged her a distance, causing serious head injuries. Her parents, who were also on the holiday, chose not to press charges against the operator, stating it was an accident. The police have registered a case of accidental death, and the British Deputy High Commission in Mumbai is providing consular assistance to the family.

Austrian designers Maya Pindeus and Johanna Pichlbauer have created a robot called the "Beautification machine" that applies makeup, specifically eyeliner and lipstick, to a person's face. The device consists of three components: a lipstick wheel, an eyeliner applicator, and a mask that illuminates when the process is complete. While the machine is intended to simplify the makeup application process, it appears to have some precision issues, as seen in a video of women testing the device. The machine's creators showcased their invention at the Biennale Internationale Design Saint-Etienne 2015 in France as part of their art project.

A former US Army enlistee, John T. Booker Jr., was arrested and charged with attempting to detonate a car bomb at Fort Riley military base in Kansas. Booker, who also used the alias Mohammed Abdullah Hassan, had previously posted on Facebook about the "adrenaline rush" of dying in jihad and had expressed plans to commit an insider attack against American soldiers. The "bomb" was actually a non-explosive device created by confidential informants, and no one was in danger. Booker had enlisted in the Army last year but was terminated after the FBI questioned him about his Facebook posts and he waived his Miranda rights, revealing his plans to commit an attack.

England's push for victory in the first Test against the West Indies was led by Gary Ballance, who scored his fourth Test century in just nine matches. However, the day's highlight belonged to Chris Jordan, who made a stunning catch to dismiss Darren Bravo, leaving West Indies at 98 for two and 340 runs short of their target. England's Alastair Cook set a challenging target of 438 for the West Indies, but they began their chase solidly, reaching 90 for one before Jordan's catch. England remains firm favorites to secure a valuable victory, but all results are still possible.

Massachusetts Senator Elizabeth Warren expressed relief that Dzhokhar Tsarnaev has been found guilty of the Boston bombing, but does not support the death penalty. Warren believes Tsarnaev should spend his life in prison without the possibility of parole, rather than being executed. She stated that her heart goes out to the families affected by the tragedy, but her stance on the death penalty remains unchanged. The case will now move on to the penalty stage of the trial, where the jury will decide whether to sentence Tsarnaev to life in prison or the death penalty.

Tokyo's Narita Airport has installed brightly-colored running tracks in its new Terminal 3 to celebrate the 2020 Olympics. The cushioned tracks, which include directional information, serve as a useful navigation tool for passengers, with blue lanes leading to departures and red lanes to arrivals. The airport opted for the running tracks to save money, instead of installing traditional moving walkways or illuminated signs. The new terminal, primarily used for low-cost carrier departures, first opened on April 8.

Hull City manager Steve Bruce is interested in signing Leeds United midfielder Alex Mowatt, who has impressed this season with nine goals. Mowatt, a 20-year-old England U20 international, has drawn attention from several Premier League clubs and was scouted by Hull during a recent match against Wolves. Bruce is keen to bolster his squad with homegrown talent, and Mowatt is one of several promising Leeds players who could be targeted by other clubs in the summer. Mowatt graduated from Leeds' academy in 2013 and has since become a key player for the first team.

Parma, a Serie A club, has been deducted four points by the Italian Football Federation (FIGC) for breaching financial regulations. This adds to the three points they were previously penalized, leaving them with a total of 12 points from 30 games. The club is currently at the bottom of Serie A and has been struggling financially, with players not being paid all season. Parma was also declared bankrupt last month.
